author: @A.Lifecraft
text:
0:50 this animation, regardless of how often it is shown, is wrong. Wings actually redirect the airflow downward and this downward accelleration of air, through conservation of momentum, contributes to lifting the wing upwards.
Thinking along these lines, wind energy is 1/2mv². If we harvest energy from a given amount m of moving air, its speed will decrease. Of course we can never go 100%, because we would then have a volume of increasing air density which would lead to air flowing off into the surrounding space... However, we can see the decrease in overall velocity as an addition of tiny local vectors in the opposite direction, in other words: turbulence. Wind turbines harvest energy by decreasing the laminarity of airflow, hence increasing its entropy.

author: @chaosopher23
text:
Wind turbine blades are already molded composites.  It would not take much to rework the molds for venturi holes.  It would also lighten the load on the horizontal bearings, and by being lighter, may extend the life of the blade significantly.  The venturis would not be optimum, but they would be very good.  A vertical might benefit from this technology.

V. 2 would make the wind come in through a snorkel and spin the blades that way, a big fan pointing up.  It wouldn't be very pretty, but far better to look at than oil mosquitoes (well pumps).

author: @Route72Trucks
text:
Glad to see you covering the wind wall again.   I was thinking on the shroud making for these.   PVC pipe is easily shaped when warm.   

Cut 4” pipe to length “or minimum ID” to length, warm in small oven, drive a cone shaped mold into one end forcing a taper and shape you want.    Cheap and easy man!

author: @Sailorman6996
text:
No, that's an old and false explanation for lift.
Modern explanation for LIFT is using the Coanda effect and one of Newton's law, and angle of attack/pitch.
Bernoulli's principal can be used for fluids flowing in an enclosed space (like a pipe) - I think not in open air.

If you build something around a wind turbine to increase it's area, you don't get "beyond" or "beat" the Betz limit. The added area need to be considered in calculations.

author: @jimwilliams1536
text:
remember that electrostatic solid state wind powered electric generator design that just uses a charged grid of wires in an air flow? the one that is the reverse application of the principles of electrostatic lifters? 
You could replace all them fans with an array of small wire grids behind each hole. Those systems suffer from maintainance issues because of corrosion. If the main generator unit is really an array of smaller wire grid components then it will be easier to maintain.

author: @SaltGrains_Fready
text:
IF there was a dimple protruding up between each hole that would streamline and accelerate the distributed flow into the holes, that would augment this process even more.
Think of this like a giant sieve in a huge drain of water moving into it. IF all the surfaces between the holes were sharpened up into points - it would cut the fluid, air in this case, rapidly with no resistance, the way a potato slicer cuts a potato into long square sliced chips.
Food for thought.

author: @davidl.howser9707
text: Robert,  3D printing with smooth PETG plastic ducts that transition from a square opening to a round opening seems would be a magnifier of surface air pressure captured, and efficiently redirect to the PC Fans props  to then strike those props at both a higher speed, and best angle of attack to transfer more mechanical energy potential to the PC Fans resulting in rotating them faster. Removable square nested ducts locked together on four of the square duct's sides that then are pressed tightly into the PC fan's board's flat surface round opening to hold the ducts tightly locked in place seems plausible to test the naked board against the board when fitted with the plethora of the square to round transitioning ducts suggested. When tightly populating the board face with ducts for each PC Fan the existing wind facing air pressure zone would be retained, BUT :  )  moved outward from the boards previously naked surface to the area of the low resistance presented edges of the square nested ducts, to then have air motion guided to the PC Fans props. Resulting in more of the approchung air, at high pressure, and at a higher speed passing over the PC Fan props. The more efficiently directed air motion onto the PC fans prop blades at volume while still retaining the wall's forward facing square area positive pressure zone explained so eloquently here seems a wind force magnifier. Conversely, a forward facing round opening cone simply pressed into the PC Fans opening would not be much of a wind force multiplier as a wind pressure would leak around the outer round duct surface edges of round ducts onto then guided across the board face to the board edges. Thank you as always for the videos. I hope that Luke is doing well adjusting to University life.

author: @ianrosswilliams
text: Oops, that's perpetuating the myth of how wings generate lift.  Although Bernoulli implies a slight drop of pressure due to the faster flow on top, most of the lift is actually just the change in momentum at the leading edge stagnation point.  Work out the upward component pressure due to the momentum change of air flow on the wing compared to the minor lift due to the speed differential top to bottom.

Great, informative video Rob. Keep inspiring us

replies.author: @CosD
replies.text:
Yes. Wings generate lift because of the angle of attack moving forwards.. so many myths surround wing theories, the shape reduces drag, a flat wing works just as well. Try it with. 3 ft length of 1/4 “ balsa , wave it about 👍🏼
Also, with Venturi shrouds , note even a straight edge (25 deg) angled Venturi worked better than a hole in various tank tests we did for tidal energy many years ago.
